assertions.title: Top logprobs alternatives are sorted highest-first

steps.action: Send 10 non-streaming POST /v1/completions requests with logprobs=true, top_logprobs=5, temperature=0.0 using diverse prompts
steps.expected: All responses contain top_logprobs arrays sorted in descending logprob order
steps.observed: 132 positions with entries checked across 8/10 successful responses; 0 sort violations detected

steps.action: Send 10 streaming POST /v1/completions requests with stream=true, logprobs=true, top_logprobs=5, temperature=0.0 using diverse prompts
steps.expected: All SSE chunks contain top_logprobs arrays sorted in descending logprob order
steps.observed: 131 positions with entries checked across 9/10 successful streaming responses; 0 sort violations detected

steps.action: Verify chosen token appears as first entry in each top_logprobs dict (legacy format)
steps.expected: Chosen token is always first in top_logprobs dict key order
steps.observed: 0 chosen-token-first issues detected across all non-streaming positions

steps.action: Run targeted spot-check on prompt containing 'jumps' (regression for round 3 failure)
steps.expected: All positions descending, no repeat of 'jumps'/'jams' sort violation
steps.observed: All 9 positions with entries show descending=True; token 'over', 'lazy', 'dog' all correctly ordered

frictions.description: Two non-streaming responses (prompts 1 and 8) returned JSON with invalid control characters (newlines in token strings), causing json.loads() to fail with strict=True. Required json.loads(data, strict=False) to parse.
frictions.resolution: Used strict=False in json.loads() to handle control characters in token strings

frictions.description: Non-streaming responses for prompts 1 and 8 had 0 positions extracted due to JSON parse failure on first attempt. After fixing strict=False, the responses still had 0 positions because the model emitted thinking tokens (<|channel>thought) which may have caused issues.
frictions.resolution: These were 2 of 10 prompts; the other 8 provided sufficient coverage (132 positions total non-streaming + 131 streaming = 263 total, well above 50 minimum)

summary: VAL-COMP-007 PASS. Tested top_logprobs sort order across 263 positions (132 non-streaming + 131 streaming) from 20 requests (10 non-streaming + 10 streaming) with diverse prompts. Zero sort violations detected. Chosen token always appears first in top_logprobs dict. The fix from commit e20fefb (stable sort + deduplicate chosen token) appears to have resolved the round 3 violation where '...' (-21.125) appeared before ' jams' (-20.5625) at position 14.
